API Tokens

Bearer tokens that let scripts and external integrations call the Stillwater API. Each token has a scope that controls what it can do (read-only, write, webhook ingress, or admin). Tokens are shown once at creation; if you lose one, revoke it and create a new one. Read more →

API tokens are long-lived credentials that let scripts and external tools call the Stillwater REST API without a browser session. Each token is scoped (read, write, webhook, or admin) so you can grant exactly the access an integration needs and revoke it independently.

No API tokens created.

Read-only access to artists, libraries, rules, and settings. Safe for dashboards and integrations that only consume data. Read more →
Permits creating and modifying artists, triggering rule fixes, and queuing background work. Does not grant access to user management or admin-only settings. Read more →
Allows the token to receive inbound webhook deliveries. Use a dedicated token with only this scope to limit blast radius if the token is ever exposed. Read more →
Combines read, write, and webhook scopes. Admin-gated routes still require the owning user to hold the administrator role. Read more →

Token created. Copy it now -- it will not be shown again.
